lessons.lessonTitle: Wetland Detectives: Finding the City's Lungs
lessons.location: Jiefang Park (解放公园), Jiang'an District
learningGoals: Identify the three main parts of a wetland: water, soil, and plants.
Use 'There is/There are' to describe nature observations in English.
Create a basic map of the park's wetland area using symbols.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will go on a scavenger hunt to find different wetland features and take photos of local plants. They will then work in teams to draw a 'Nature Map' showing how water moves through the park.
lessons.safetyNotes: Stay on the designated wooden boardwalks; do not touch unknown berries or fungi; keep a safe distance from the water edge.

lessons.location: East Lake - Tingtao Scenic Area (东湖听涛景区)
learningGoals: Measure water transparency using a DIY Secchi disk.
Explain the process of natural filtration using sequence adverbs (First, Then, Finally).
Compare water samples from different locations in the lake.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will collect water samples and test their clarity. They will build a small-scale model of a natural filter using sand, gravel, and charcoal to see how wetlands clean our water.
lessons.safetyNotes: Wear waterproof gloves when handling lake water; use the 'buddy system' near the docks; do not drink the lake water.

lessons.location: Moon Lake Park (月湖公园), Hanyang District
learningGoals: Describe how lotus leaves stay dry using the 'Lotus Effect'.
Use comparative adjectives (bigger, smoother, greener) to describe wetland plants.
Design a waterproof product inspired by plant structures.
lessons.activitySummary: Students will use magnifying glasses to inspect lotus leaves and aquatic plants. They will perform a 'water drop test' on different surfaces and sketch an invention based on plant adaptations.
lessons.safetyNotes: Be mindful of high temperatures and stay in the shade; apply mosquito repellent; avoid leaning over the stone railings.

lessons.lessonTitle: Winged Neighbors: Bird Watching and Food Chains
lessons.location: Sha Lake Park (沙湖公园), Wuchang District
learningGoals: Identify three common wetland birds using binoculars.
Build a simple food chain model showing energy flow from plants to birds.
Practice using action verbs to describe bird behaviors (flying, diving, nesting).
lessons.activitySummary: Using binoculars, students will conduct a bird census and record their findings in a field journal. They will then participate in a role-play game to understand how energy moves through the wetland food web.
lessons.safetyNotes: Use binocular neck straps at all times; keep noise levels low to avoid disturbing wildlife; watch for slippery leaves on the paths.
